reasons: Karrie directly punishes Baxia's high durability and frontline role.
Baxia can reduce sustain, but he cannot stop Karrie's core true-damage threat if she is protected.

proof.summary: Baxia wants to absorb damage as a tank/frontline, while Karrie's Lightwheel Mark deals repeated max-HP true damage.
worksBestWhen: Karrie has peel and enough space to stack marks.
Baxia is forced to frontline instead of reaching Karrie directly.
failureCases: Baxia reaches Karrie with teammate follow-up.
Karrie is zoned before she can trigger Lightwheel Mark.

reasons: Lunox can pressure Baxia with target-HP scaling magic damage.
This makes her valuable into Baxia's tanky frontline style.

proof.summary: Baxia wants to survive through durability and damage reduction, while Lunox can repeatedly threaten him with Chaos Assault's target-HP damage.
worksBestWhen: Lunox stays out of Baxia's direct engage path.
Baxia's mobility tool has already been used.
failureCases: Baxia reaches Lunox before she can kite.
Lunox gets forced to spend defensive tools before damaging Baxia.

reasons: Dyrroth can pressure Baxia with aggressive trades and defense-reduction style damage.
This is strongest in isolated skirmishes before Baxia's teamfight utility fully matters.

proof.summary: Baxia wants to absorb frontline pressure, while Dyrroth can force sharp burst trades that reduce Baxia's comfort in close-range duels.
worksBestWhen: Dyrroth fights with rage or enhanced skill timing ready.
Baxia is isolated away from team follow-up.
failureCases: Dyrroth takes a long fight into Baxia's team.
Baxia disengages and waits for backup.

reasons: Valir can slow and knock Baxia back before Baxia reaches priority targets.
Baxia is less effective if he spends his mobility only to be pushed away.

proof.summary: Baxia wants to roll or run into the enemy formation, while Valir can push him away and deny clean engage timing.
worksBestWhen: Valir saves Searing Torrent for Baxia's entry.
The team uses Valir's disengage to reposition.
failureCases: Valir's knockback is used too early.

reasons: Lesley can chip Baxia from range and threaten damage that ignores defensive value.
This works best as a late-game spacing counter, not as a direct early brawl.

proof.summary: Baxia wants to absorb frontline damage, while Lesley's true-damage sniper pattern can punish tanks from safer range.
worksBestWhen: Baxia cannot directly roll onto Lesley.
failureCases: Baxia reaches Lesley with team follow-up.
Lesley is forced into early fights before her damage is online.
